headline: Oracle And Google Cloud Link AI To Keep Enterprise Data On Oracle
summary: Oracle (NYSE:ORCL) expanded its AI partnership with Google Cloud, adding new database level capabilities for joint customers. The agreement includes the Oracle AI Database Agent for Gemini Enterprise, enabling natural language access to Oracle data. The collaboration supports multi cloud AI workflows that use Oracle databases together with Google Cloud AI services. For investors watching NYSE:ORCL, this update sits at the intersection of cloud infrastructure, data management, and enterprise...

content.title: Oracle And Google Cloud Link AI To Keep Enterprise Data On Oracle

The collaboration supports multi cloud AI workflows that use Oracle databases together with Google Cloud AI services. For investors watching NYSE:ORCL, this update sits at the intersection of cloud infrastructure, data management, and enterprise...

headline: Applied Digital signs $7.5 billion AI data center lease with US hyperscaler
summary: Applied Digital has signed a long-term lease worth $7.5 billion with an unnamed U.S.-based hyperscaler at its new ‌Delta Forge 1 site, it said on Thursday, strengthening its ‌position as a data center provider for artificial intelligence workloads.  The 15-year lease covers 300 megawatts of computing capacity at the 430‑megawatt Delta Forge 1 site in southern U.S., the company said.  The deal makes the customer Applied Digital's second ‌U.S.-based investment‑grade hyperscale tenant ⁠across its data center sites and lifts total contracted lease revenue to more than $23 billion.

content.title: Applied Digital signs $7.5 billion AI data center lease with US hyperscaler
content.summary: Applied Digital has signed a long-term lease worth $7.5 billion with an unnamed U.S.-based hyperscaler at its new ‌Delta Forge 1 site, it said on Thursday, strengthening its ‌position as a data center provider for artificial intelligence workloads.  The 15-year lease covers 300 megawatts of computing capacity at the 430‑megawatt Delta Forge 1 site in southern U.S., the company said.  The deal makes the customer Applied Digital's second ‌U.S.-based investment‑grade hyperscale tenant ⁠across its data center sites and lifts total contracted lease revenue to more than $23 billion.

headline: Oracle stock: Morgan Stanley says questions on margins and financing remain open
summary: Investing.com -- Morgan Stanley trimmed its price target on Oracle to $207 from $213 per share in a note Thursday, maintaining an Equal Weight rating as improved execution and broadening demand are offset by unresolved questions around the cost and margin profile of the company's rapidly expanding GPU-as-a-service business.

content.title: Oracle stock: Morgan Stanley says questions on margins and financing remain open
content.summary: Investing.com -- Morgan Stanley trimmed its price target on Oracle to $207 from $213 per share in a note Thursday, maintaining an Equal Weight rating as improved execution and broadening demand are offset by unresolved questions around the cost and margin profile of the company's rapidly expanding GPU-as-a-service business.

headline: Super Micro drops 10% after reported loss of major Oracle contract
summary: Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ:SMCI) shares fell 10% on Thursday following reports that the company lost a sizable contract with Oracle involving Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) GB300 NVL72 server racks. Contract cancellation worth over $1 billionAccording to Bluefin Research, Oracle canceled between 300 and 400 racks previously ordered from Super Micro, with each unit valued at roughly $3.

content.summary: Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ:SMCI) shares fell 10% on Thursday following reports that the company lost a sizable contract with Oracle involving Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) GB300 NVL72 server racks. Contract cancellation worth over $1 billionAccording to Bluefin Research, Oracle canceled between 300 and 400 racks previously ordered from Super Micro, with each unit valued at roughly $3.

headline: Meta slashes 8,000 jobs, or 10% of its workforce, as Microsoft offers buyouts
summary: Meta is laying off about 8,000 workers, or about 10% of its workforce, the company said Thursday as it continues to ramp up spending on artificial intelligence infrastructure and highly paid AI-expert hires.  Also Thursday, Microsoft said it was offering voluntary buyouts to thousands of its U.S. employees.  While an alternative to the sudden layoffs removing tech workers from peers like Meta and Oracle, the savings are likely tied to a similar industry upheaval that is requiring huge spending on the costs of artificial intelligence.

content.title: Meta slashes 8,000 jobs, or 10% of its workforce, as Microsoft offers buyouts
content.summary: Meta is laying off about 8,000 workers, or about 10% of its workforce, the company said Thursday as it continues to ramp up spending on artificial intelligence infrastructure and highly paid AI-expert hires.  Also Thursday, Microsoft said it was offering voluntary buyouts to thousands of its U.S. employees.  While an alternative to the sudden layoffs removing tech workers from peers like Meta and Oracle, the savings are likely tied to a similar industry upheaval that is requiring huge spending on the costs of artificial intelligence.

headline: Super Micro Computer Stock Plunges on Lost Oracle Contract, but Supply Chain Excesses Could Be the Bigger Problem for SMCI
